content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer NNN, find sum of its digits.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer NNN.


Output Format

Output on a single line: the sum of digits of NNN.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the sum of the decimal digits of N on a separate line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Digit sum of 2 is 2; 123456789 has digits 1 through 9, sum=45

solution.approach: The digit sum of a number can be obtained by repeatedly extracting its last digit using the modulo operator and adding it to an accumulator. After removing the last digit by integer division by 10, the process is repeated until the number becomes zero. This runs in time proportional to the number of digits, which is at most 10 for the given constraints, so it is easily fast enough. The algorithm is applied independently for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of digits) = O(T * log10 N) per input, which is at most O(1000) operations for the maximal constraints.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (ignoring input and output storage).
